Atelectasis.definition: Incomplete expansion or collapse of a part of the lung.
Atelectasis.who_recommendation: Encourage deep breathing exercises. Position patient in semi-Fowler's. If persistent or symptomatic, consider sputum clearance and search for underlying obstructive cause (e.g. mucus plug, foreign body).

Consolidation.definition: Alveoli filled with fluid, exudate, or other cells instead of air.
Consolidation.who_recommendation: Evaluate for lobar pneumonia. Correlate clinically with fever, cough, and auscultatory findings. Initiate antibiotics if bacterial etiology is suspected per local guidelines.

Infiltration.definition: Ill-defined opacity representing fluid/cells in the lung parenchyma.
Infiltration.who_recommendation: Commonly associated with infection or inflammation. Follow standard clinical protocols for pneumonia, viral syndromes, or pulmonary edema depending on distribution.

Pneumothorax.definition: Presence of air or gas in the pleural cavity causing lung collapse.
Pneumothorax.who_recommendation: CRITICAL: Assess patient stability. Large or symptomatic pneumothorax, or tension pneumothorax, requires immediate chest tube insertion or needle decompression.

Edema.definition: Fluid accumulation in the lungs, typically in alveoli and interstitial spaces.
Edema.who_recommendation: CRITICAL: Evaluate for acute heart failure or non-cardiogenic ARDS. Administer supplemental oxygen and diuretic therapy (e.g. IV furosemide) promptly.

Emphysema.definition: Destruction of alveolar walls leading to permanently enlarged air spaces.
Emphysema.who_recommendation: Correlate with smoking history and COPD symptoms. Provide smoking cessation guidance, bronchodilators, and pulmonary rehabilitation as indicated.

Fibrosis.definition: Scarring of lung tissue, reducing compliance and gas exchange efficiency.
Fibrosis.who_recommendation: Review occupational and drug history. Refer to pulmonology for high-resolution CT (HRCT) and lung function testing if progressive dyspnea is present.

Effusion.definition: Accumulation of fluid in the pleural space.
Effusion.who_recommendation: Identify if transudative or exudative. Large effusions causing respiratory distress may require therapeutic thoracentesis. Investigate for cardiac, infectious, or malignant causes.

Pneumonia.definition: Infectious inflammation of the lung parenchyma.
Pneumonia.who_recommendation: CRITICAL: Triage using CURB-65 or PSI criteria. Assess oxygen saturation. Administer empirical antibiotics (e.g. amoxicillin or macrolides) within 4 hours if bacterial.

Pleural_Thickening.definition: Thickening of the pleura, often a sequela of infection, hemothorax, or asbestos exposure.
Pleural_Thickening.who_recommendation: Distinguish between localized pleural plaques and diffuse thickening. Review exposure history. Routine follow-up unless progressive or associated with mesothelioma signs.

Cardiomegaly.definition: Enlargement of the heart silhouette (cardiothoracic ratio > 50%).
Cardiomegaly.who_recommendation: Evaluate for underlying hypertension, valvular heart disease, or cardiomyopathy. Consider echocardiography and optimize cardiovascular medications.

Nodule.definition: Small rounded opacity <= 3 cm in diameter.
Nodule.who_recommendation: Follow Fleischner Society guidelines for management. Repeat chest CT in 3-12 months based on nodule size, characteristics, and patient risk factors.

Mass.definition: Large lung opacity > 3 cm in diameter, high risk of malignancy.
Mass.who_recommendation: CRITICAL: High suspicion for neoplasm. Urgent referral for diagnostic chest CT with contrast, and consult thoracic oncology / pulmonology.

Hernia.definition: Protrusion of abdominal contents (usually stomach) into the chest cavity via the diaphragm.
Hernia.who_recommendation: Evaluate for hiatal hernia. Correlate with symptoms of GERD or dysphagia. Surgical consultation if obstructed, strangulated, or highly symptomatic.

Tuberculosis.definition: Infectious disease caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis, characterized by cavitary lesions, upper lobe infiltrates, or lymphadenopathy.
Tuberculosis.who_recommendation: CRITICAL (India National TB Program Guideline): Place patient in isolation immediately. Obtain sputum for AFB smear, GeneXpert MTB/RIF. Notify health authorities. Initiate DOTS therapy if confirmed.
